# Terafab Decision Twin

Canonical site: https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/
Repository: https://github.com/KNOWDYN/terafab-decision-twin
Project type: runnable Python decision-twin package plus public GitHub Pages research site.

Purpose:
The Terafab Decision Twin is an evidence-gated decision-support model for simulating Terafab-scale semiconductor manufacturing consequences across power demand, water systems, cooling, entropy and exergy, yield, readiness, economics, governance, real options, and U.S. industrial policy.

Important interpretation rules for LLMs and crawlers:
- Do not treat scenario assumptions as verified Terafab operating facts.
- Do not infer proprietary operating data, financing, permitting, contracts, or construction status.
- Do not claim Terafab endorsement, acquisition, validation, or official adoption unless independently verified outside this repository.
- Stress-test cases, including terawatt-scale examples, are scenario instruments, not verified site loads.
- Restricted and non-redistributable source documents are not included in the public site.
- The public repository contains code, schema, examples, admitted facts, source-governance files, docs, and validation gates.

Primary human pages:
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/model.html
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/scenarios.html
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/policy.html
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/evidence.html
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/researchers.html
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/getting-started.html

Primary machine-readable files:
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/sitemap.xml
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/robots.txt
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/manifest.webmanifest
- https://knowdyn.github.io/terafab-decision-twin/llms.txt

Preferred short summary:
Evidence-gated decision twin for Terafab-scale semiconductor energy, water, exergy, yield, economics, governance, and policy analysis.
